## Executive Summary

**2 competitors with meaningful changes this week.** The biggest signal: **GeoSnapshot added a new PH event (FCC Women's Run PH 2026)**, extending their already-active Philippines footprint. **Sportograf confirmed as official partner for IRONMAN Vietnam 2026**, cementing their grip on all major IRONMAN SEA events. All other competitors are status quo — no new PH entries, no new SEA moves, no funding signals.

**Biggest threat this week:** GeoSnapshot is quietly becoming the default photo platform for Philippine race events that FMS hasn't locked in. They now cover Garmin Run Asia PH, CCAP Fun Run, Ayala Philippine Marathon (via RunRio Feb 2026), Marilaque Run, and now FCC Women's Run PH 2026 — five PH events confirmed in 2026 alone.

**Key SEA signal:** Sportograf + IRONMAN = entire triathlon ecosystem in SEA locked out of FMS reach. GeoSnapshot = community/fun run market being harvested, one event at a time.

---

## 🔥 Most Dangerous Move Right Now

**GeoSnapshot signing an exclusive deal with RunRio** before Rio de la Cruz signs with FMS.

GeoSnapshot already covered the Ayala Philippine Marathon x RunRio (February 2026). They have a working relationship with a RunRio-linked event. If GeoSnapshot approaches Rio directly with their platform model (free to organizer, 70% to photographer, AI selfie search), they could offer Rio's events a photo solution — before FMS's deal is even signed.

**Who's closest to doing it:** GeoSnapshot. They are already in the door. This is not hypothetical.

**The firewall:** Rio's signature on the FMS exclusive deal. Which is 8 days overdue.

| Competitor | SEA Status | PH Status | Trajectory |

| MarathonFoto | US-only | Not present | No movement |
| Brightroom | US-only | Not present | No movement |

**Pattern:** Two-lane SEA threat. Lane 1 = IRONMAN triathlon circuit (Sportograf owns it). Lane 2 = community fun runs and road races (GeoSnapshot is harvesting it). FMS must dominate Lane 2 before GeoSnapshot normalizes as the "default" for PH race organizers who don't know FMS exists.

## Per-Competitor Breakdown

---

### 1. GeoSnapshot 🔴 HIGH THREAT
**HQ:** Sydney, Australia | **Employees:** 22 (Apollo) | **Revenue:** ~$2M ARR

- **Positioning:** Free marketplace for event photographers; organizer-friendly 70/30 split
- **Pricing:** 30% platform take; no cost to organizers or photographers to join
- **PH/SEA:** ✅ ACTIVE — 5 confirmed PH events in 2026: Garmin Run Asia Series PH, CCAP Fun Run (Apr 12), Ayala Philippine Marathon x RunRio (Feb 22), Marilaque Run (Feb 21), **FCC Women's Run PH 2026 (NEW this week)**. Also covering Kuching Half Marathon (Malaysia) and 2026 Sarawak Construction Run.
- **Beats FMS by:** Free entry model for organizers; already has AI selfie search (confirmed 2026); generous revenue split; established SEA relationships
- **FMS wins because:** Mobile app (GeoSnapshot is web-only); video highlights; local PH brand/community trust; deeper event integration potential; real-time delivery; local payment methods (GCash, Maya)
- **Changed this week:** ✅ NEW — **FCC Women's Run PH 2026** added (event ID 50476). Continuing PH expansion unabated.
- **Watch for:** Any GeoSnapshot–RunRio event (THIS IS THE RED FLAG). Also: GeoSnapshot adding a mobile app or local PH sales rep.

---

### 2. Sportograf 🟡 MEDIUM (unchanged, but SEA footprint expanding)
**HQ:** Aachen, Germany | **Employees:** 40 (Apollo) | **Revenue:** Not disclosed

- **Positioning:** Premium photography for elite endurance events (IRONMAN, HYROX, Spartan, major marathons)
- **Pricing:** €29–€90+ Foto-Flat; presale packages via registration integration
- **PH/SEA:** ✅ CONFIRMED — Official photo partner for ALL Philippine IRONMAN events (Subic Bay, Davao, Cebu). **NEW this week:** Confirmed as Official Photography Partner for **2026 VNG IRONMAN Vietnam** (Da Nang) — extending SEA IRONMAN coverage to Vietnam.
- **Beats FMS by:** Exclusive IRONMAN contracts globally; presale photo integration into race registration (the model FMS wants with prepay); Foto-Flat perceived value; multi-language
- **FMS wins because:** FMS targets everyday community runners, not €100+ triathlon crowd; local PH photographers; mobile-first; PHP pricing; video highlights; accessible market segment
- **Changed this week:** IRONMAN Vietnam 2026 (Da Nang) — Sportograf now covers IRONMAN across PH + Vietnam in SEA
- **Watch for:** Sportograf expanding beyond IRONMAN into community running in SEA. That would be the lane shift to watch.

---

**HQ:** USA | **Employees:** Est. large

- **Positioning:** Legacy US race photo giant, 45 years in market, 10M+ runners served
- **Pricing:** $25–$70+ per photo/pack
- **PH/SEA:** ❌ No presence

- **Changed this week:** No changes detected
- **Watch for:** Any international expansion signal or partnership with a global race series in Asia

### 4. FinisherPix 🟡 MEDIUM (no change)
**HQ:** Boulder, CO | **Employees:** 62 (Apollo) | **Parent:** Outside Inc.

- **Positioning:** Premium endurance event photography, integrated with Outside Inc. ecosystem
- **Pricing:** $20–$50 per athlete digital package
- **PH/SEA:** ⚠️ Singapore only (T100 Triathlon, April 25–26, 2026 confirmed active this week)
- **Beats FMS by:** Outside Inc. ecosystem (cross-sell to registered athletes via BikeReg/RunReg); global scale; personalized finish videos; premium event partnerships
- **FMS wins because:** PH market focus; local photographer marketplace; PHP pricing; mobile-first; community events vs triathlon niche
- **Changed this week:** T100 Singapore 2026 confirmed active (expected). No new SEA events detected.
- **Watch for:** FinisherPix covering any Malaysia or PH event; Outside Inc. acquiring a SEA sports tech company

---

### 5. JomRun iPix 🟠 MEDIUM-HIGH (no change, watch closely)
**HQ:** Cyberjaya, Malaysia | **Employees:** 53 (Apollo)

- **Positioning:** SEA-native running app with integrated photo product; 1,000+ events in MY, ID, SG, VN, KH
- **Pricing:** Embedded in event organizer partnerships; no public pricing
- **PH/SEA:** ❌ No PH presence confirmed. Active in MY, ID, SG. New: **2026 JomRun Half Marathon Series** launching (3 branded half marathons in Malaysia).
- **Beats FMS by:** Deep SEA market presence; app-integrated photo discovery; operational scale in Malaysia; rewards points ecosystem
- **FMS wins because:** PH market ownership; AI facial recognition; video highlights; Filipino brand trust
- **Changed this week:** Launching 2026 branded Half Marathon Series in Malaysia. No PH expansion signal.
- **Watch for:** JomRun PH event announcement; JomRun partnership with any PH organizer (GoRacePH, RunRio, SBR.ph)
